coreCompetencies: Basic understanding of how text is represented numerically for machine learning
Elementary grasp of tokenization, splitting text into words and subword units
Ability to clean and preprocess text, handling casing, punctuation and stopwords
Basic notion of word embeddings and why similar words sit close together
Ability to use libraries such as spaCy and Hugging Face under supervision
Elementary practice running sentiment analysis and text classification examples
Basic familiarity with part-of-speech tags and named entities
Ability to read model outputs and inspect predictions on sample text
complementaryCompetencies: Basic Python and comfort working with text files
Familiarity with regular expressions for pattern matching
Elementary sense of linguistic corpora and datasets
indicators: Requires constant supervision
Can implement basic text preprocessing with guidance
Needs 6-12 months of mentoring

coreCompetencies: Ability to fine-tune pretrained language models for classification tasks
Practical grasp of the transformer architecture and self-attention
Ability to build named entity recognition and part-of-speech tagging pipelines
Familiarity with subword tokenization such as byte-pair encoding and WordPiece
Ability to train and evaluate text classifiers with precision, recall and F1
Practice with topic modeling and clustering of documents
Ability to set up basic machine translation and speech-to-text from existing models
Grasp of train, validation and test splits and overfitting in text tasks
complementaryCompetencies: Familiarity with pretrained models such as the BERT and GPT families
Ability to work with multilingual datasets and tokenizers
Sense of dialogue and question-answering datasets
indicators: Develops NLP components with supervision
Implements standard models on well-defined tasks
Optimizes basic preprocessing and training pipelines

coreCompetencies: Command of transformer architectures and how attention captures context
Ability to fine-tune and adapt large language models for domain tasks
Ability to build embedding-based semantic search and retrieval
Solid command of evaluation metrics including BLEU, ROUGE and perplexity
Ability to design text generation systems with different decoding strategies
Practice handling long documents and context length limits
Ability to build entity and relation extraction pipelines
Awareness of bias and fairness concerns in language data and models
complementaryCompetencies: Familiarity with neural machine translation architectures
Ability to work with speech recognition and synthesis
Sense of data annotation workflows and inter-annotator agreement
indicators: Designs complete NLP systems with minimal supervision
Leads focused language processing projects
Improves model quality through systematic evaluation

coreCompetencies: Ability to architect production NLP pipelines from ingestion to serving
Command of model efficiency techniques such as distillation and quantization
Ability to deploy large language models with acceptable latency and cost
Solid command of retrieval-augmented generation grounded in enterprise data
Ability to build multilingual systems that share models across languages
Ability to design offline and online evaluation for language models
Practice monitoring for data drift and quality regressions in production
Ability to mentor engineers on NLP design and evaluation
complementaryCompetencies: Familiarity with vector databases and approximate nearest neighbor search
Ability to design annotation and data curation strategies at scale
Sense of prompt design and in-context learning for language models
indicators: Leads development of production NLP features
Mentors junior engineers on language systems
Makes informed architectural decisions on NLP pipelines

coreCompetencies: Expertise in designing NLP platforms shared across multiple products
Ability to establish evaluation-driven development for language models
Command of model selection between open and proprietary language models
Ability to build data pipelines that feed continuous model improvement
Solid command of scaling inference for high-volume text workloads
Ability to design guardrails against hallucination and unsafe generation
Expertise in cost and latency optimization across the NLP stack
Ability to define evaluation benchmarks tailored to business tasks
complementaryCompetencies: Familiarity with reinforcement learning from human feedback for alignment
Ability to design human-in-the-loop review for generated text
Sense of privacy and compliance for text containing personal data
indicators: Designs NLP architectures for complete products
Defines team standards for language model development
Leads cross-team initiatives on NLP infrastructure

coreCompetencies: Ability to architect enterprise NLP platforms serving many teams
Expertise in governance frameworks for deploying language models
Command of evaluation infrastructure and continuous quality monitoring
Ability to design language systems that meet reliability and safety targets
Solid command of security threats specific to language models such as prompt injection
Ability to lead adoption of NLP across products and business units
Expertise in managing the cost of large-scale language model serving
Ability to set data governance policy for text and training corpora
complementaryCompetencies: Familiarity with emerging model architectures and their trade-offs
Ability to design shared embedding and retrieval services
Sense of on-device and edge deployment of compact models
indicators: Architects NLP solutions for multiple teams
Influences organizational decisions on language technology
Represents the company technically in the NLP domain

coreCompetencies: Technical leadership of NLP strategy across an organization
Ability to design language architectures that outlive individual models
Expertise in model-agnostic and vendor-agnostic NLP platforms
Ability to define multi-year roadmaps for language capabilities
Solid command of the total cost and economics of language systems
Expertise in building and scaling NLP engineering teams
Ability to set evaluation and safety standards adopted across the organization
Ability to guide build-versus-buy decisions for language models
complementaryCompetencies: Ability to evaluate NLP vendors and research for adoption
Familiarity with research trends in language modeling
Ability to represent NLP work at technical conferences
indicators: Defines organizational technical strategy for NLP
Recognized as a technical authority in language processing
Speaks at industry conferences on NLP

coreCompetencies: Strategic direction for how the organization builds and operates language systems
Ability to align NLP investments with product and business goals
Expertise in technical due diligence for NLP products and acquisitions
Ability to set the engineering bar for language model quality and safety
Solid command of the trade-offs between in-house and external language models
Ability to build and lead NLP engineering organizations
Expertise in translating research advances into production language systems
Ability to advise leadership on risk and opportunity in language technology
complementaryCompetencies: Ability to shape data strategy for training and evaluating language models
Familiarity with regulatory considerations for automated language processing
Ability to mentor senior engineers and technical leads
indicators: Shapes the technical direction of NLP across the company
Advises leadership on language technology decisions
Mentors senior technical leaders

coreCompetencies: Executive leadership of language technology strategy
Ability to align NLP capabilities with company strategy and roadmap
Expertise in planning and managing budgets for language model programs
Ability to set organizational policy for responsible language systems
Solid command of risk management for language models in regulated settings
Ability to build competitive advantage through NLP capabilities
Expertise in partnering with model and infrastructure providers
Ability to lead the organization through shifts in language model technology
complementaryCompetencies: Ability to represent the company on language technology matters
Familiarity with the vendor and research landscape for language models
Ability to shape hiring and organizational design for NLP teams

Accountable for the outcomes of NLP investments
